sections.paragraph:
terror, trembling, Psal. 12:9 . Root זָלַל. [“ἅπαξ λεγόμ. prop. a shaking, trembling, earthquake, see the root in Niphal. Hence a storm, a tempest. Ps. 12:9 , “the wicked walk on every side, כְּרוּם זֻלּוּת לִבְנֵי אָדָם like the rising of a tempest upon the sons of men.” Ges. add.]

זַלְזַל only in pl. זַלְזִלִּים m. shoots, twigs, sprigs, from their trembling and quivering motion, Isa 18:5. Root זָלַל, see especially Niphal. Comp. also סַלְסִלֹּוח, סַנְסִנִּים, תַּלְתַּלִּים.
